title: S3 Objects, Keys, Metadata, Tags, Listing, and Consistency · Runtime Contract: Key Prefix Design
summary: A team encodes tenant authority in free-form key strings, overwrites metadata accidentally, assumes folders are directories, and scans one unpaginated listing as a transaction. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the key prefix design increment in S3 object-model and listing invariant table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Design opaque or structured keys with explicit delimiter, normalization, tenant, cardinality, migration, and disclosure rules.
Distinguish bucket, key, version ID, delete marker, upload ID, part number, and application identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Key Prefix Design · Theory Key Prefix Design
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 6850 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate object key, version, pagination, and strong-read evidence; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design opaque or structured keys with explicit delimiter, normalization, tenant, cardinality, migration, and disclosure rules.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Key Prefix Design contract for the supplied data.
